title: Science & Research
topics: scientific method · research vocabulary · hypothesis · findings
description: Vědecká a výzkumná slovní zásoba pro akademické a profesní prostředí.
notes: ⚠️  Correlation ≠ causation: two things happening together doesn't mean one causes the other
✓ 'The study SUGGESTS that...' (hedging — not proven definitively)
✓ 'Further research IS NEEDED to...' (standard academic phrase)

examples.en: The hypothesis was tested using a randomised controlled trial.
examples.cz: Hypotéza byla testována pomocí randomizované kontrolované studie.

examples.en: The findings suggest a strong correlation between sleep and productivity.
examples.cz: Výsledky naznačují silnou korelaci mezi spánkem a produktivitou.

examples.en: The study was peer-reviewed before publication.
examples.cz: Studie prošla recenzním řízením před zveřejněním.

examples.en: We must be careful not to confuse correlation with causation.
examples.cz: Musíme být opatrní, abychom nezaměňovali korelaci s příčinností.

quiz.sentence: The results ___ that exercise reduces stress. (suggest)
A larger ___ size would make the study more reliable.
The paper was ___ in Nature magazine.
Just because X follows Y doesn't mean Y ___ X.

tasks: 1. Read a scientific abstract in English and identify: hypothesis, method, findings
2. Write a 100-word paragraph summarising a study you know about
3. Explain the difference between correlation and causation with an example
